Belgrade Biomarkers Workshop

M. Davids

27 March, 2026

  • 1 load and modify data
    • 1.1 load RDS
    • 1.2 Subset data
    • 1.3 aggregate on genus level
    • 1.4 rarefy
    • 1.5 transform
  • 2 Univariate
    • 2.1 wilcox
    • 2.2 t.test
    • 2.3 Correlation
    • 2.4 Ancombc
  • 3 Mutivariate
    • 3.1 RF factor
    • 3.2 RF continous
  • 4 Corrplot
  • 5 heatmap
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsignif)
library(ggpubr)
library(ape)
library(phytools)
library(reshape2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(gghalves)
library(tidyverse)
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
library(pROC)
library(ranger)
library(microbiome)

1 load and modify data

1.1 load RDS

ps <- readRDS("ps.16S.RDS")
# ps <- readRDS("ps.ec.RDS")
# ps <- readRDS("ps.pw.RDS")
ps
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 19469 taxa and 1000 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1000 samples by 8 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 19469 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
## phy_tree()    Phylogenetic Tree: [ 19469 tips and 19468 internal nodes ]

1.2 Subset data

ps.sub <- prune_samples(ps@sam_data$Ethnicity==1, ps)
ps.sub
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 19469 taxa and 448 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 448 samples by 8 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 19469 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
## phy_tree()    Phylogenetic Tree: [ 19469 tips and 19468 internal nodes ]

1.3 aggregate on genus level

ps.genus <- microbiome::aggregate_taxa(ps, "Genus")
ps.genus
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 359 taxa and 1000 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1000 samples by 8 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 359 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]

1.4 rarefy

# ps.rare <- rarefy_even_depth(ps, sample.size = 10000, rngseed = 123, replace = F)
# ps.rare

1.5 transform

ps.Z <- microbiome::transform(ps, "Z")
ps.Z
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 19469 taxa and 1000 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 1000 samples by 8 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 19469 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
## phy_tree()    Phylogenetic Tree: [ 19469 tips and 19468 internal nodes ]

2 Univariate

2.1 wilcox

covariate_wc <- function(ps, covariate, taxa=NULL, p.adjust="BH"){
  if (sum(is.na(get(covariate,ps@sam_data)))!=0){ps <- prune_samples(!is.na(get(covariate,ps@sam_data)), ps)}
  if (!is.null(taxa)){ps <- prune_taxa(taxa, ps)}
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)){otu_table(ps) <- otu_table(t(ps@otu_table), taxa_are_rows = F)}
  res <- lapply(taxa_names(ps), function(x) wilcox.test(ps@otu_table[,x]@.Data~get(covariate,ps@sam_data)))
  df <- data.frame(row.names=taxa_names(ps),
                   statistic=unname(unlist(lapply(res, function(x) x$statistic))),
                   p.value=unlist(lapply(res, function(x) x$p.value)))
  df$p.adjust <- p.adjust(df$p.value, method = p.adjust)
  return (df)
}

taxa2test <- taxa_sums(ps) %>% sort(decreasing = T) %>% head(n=250) %>% names()
wc.res <- covariate_wc(ps, covariate = "PPI", taxa = taxa2test)
wc.res <- cbind(wc.res, ps@tax_table[rownames(wc.res),])
wc.res %>% arrange(p.value) %>% head()
##         statistic      p.value     p.adjust  Kingdom         Phylum
## Zotu128   17730.5 1.939946e-16 4.849864e-14 Bacteria     Firmicutes
## Zotu24    19439.5 5.736608e-14 7.170760e-12 Bacteria     Firmicutes
## Zotu229   23523.5 3.133256e-10 2.611047e-08 Bacteria     Firmicutes
## Zotu109   26478.5 1.500053e-06 9.375330e-05 Bacteria Proteobacteria
## Zotu155   48142.0 1.327796e-04 6.638978e-03 Bacteria     Firmicutes
## Zotu114   30567.0 1.786140e-04 7.442252e-03 Bacteria Proteobacteria
##                       Class             Order             Family         Genus
## Zotu128             Bacilli   Lactobacillales   Streptococcaceae Streptococcus
## Zotu24              Bacilli   Lactobacillales   Streptococcaceae Streptococcus
## Zotu229       Negativicutes   Selenomonadales    Veillonellaceae   Veillonella
## Zotu109 Gammaproteobacteria    Pasteurellales    Pasteurellaceae   Haemophilus
## Zotu155          Clostridia     Clostridiales    Lachnospiraceae          <NA>
## Zotu114 Gammaproteobacteria Enterobacteriales Enterobacteriaceae    Klebsiella
##         Species
## Zotu128    <NA>
## Zotu24     <NA>
## Zotu229    <NA>
## Zotu109    <NA>
## Zotu155    <NA>
## Zotu114    <NA>
psm <- psmelt(prune_taxa((wc.res %>% arrange(p.value) %>% head() %>% rownames()), ps))

ggplot(psm, aes(x=PPI, y=Abundance)) +
  #geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~OTU+Genus) + 
  stat_compare_means() +
  gghalves::geom_half_point() +
  gghalves::geom_half_violin() + 
  scale_y_log10() + 
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x="PPI use") + 
  NULL

2.2 t.test

covariate_tt <- function(ps, covariate, taxa=NULL, p.adjust="BH"){
  if (sum(is.na(get(covariate,ps@sam_data)))!=0){ps <- prune_samples(!is.na(get(covariate,ps@sam_data)), ps)}
  if (!is.null(taxa)){ps <- prune_taxa(taxa, ps)}
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)){otu_table(ps) <- otu_table(t(ps@otu_table), taxa_are_rows = F)}
  res <- lapply(taxa_names(ps), function(x) t.test(ps@otu_table[,x]@.Data~get(covariate,ps@sam_data)))
  df <- data.frame(row.names=taxa_names(ps),
                   statistic=unname(unlist(lapply(res, function(x) x$statistic))),
                   p.value=unlist(lapply(res, function(x) x$p.value)))
  df$p.adjust <- p.adjust(df$p.value, method = p.adjust)
  return (df)
}

2.3 Correlation

covariate_cor <- function(ps, covariate, taxa=NULL, p.adjust="BH"){
  # class(get(covariate,ps@sam_data))=="numeric" # check numeric
  # taxa in phyloseq?
  
  # modify phyloseq according to parameters.
  if (sum(is.na(get(covariate,ps@sam_data)))!=0){ps <- prune_samples(!is.na(get(covariate,ps@sam_data)), ps)}
  if (!is.null(taxa)){ps <- prune_taxa(taxa, ps)}
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps)){otu_table(ps) <- otu_table(t(ps@otu_table), taxa_are_rows = F)}
  
  #
  asvcor <- lapply(taxa_names(ps), function(x) cor.test(get(covariate,ps@sam_data), method = "spearman",ps@otu_table[,x]@.Data, exact = F))
  df <- data.frame(row.names=taxa_names(ps),
                   rho=unname(unlist(lapply(asvcor, function(x) x$estimate))),
                   p.value=unlist(lapply(asvcor, function(x) x$p.value)))
  df$p.adjust <- p.adjust(df$p.value, method = p.adjust)
  return (df)
}

taxa2test <- taxa_sums(ps) %>% sort(decreasing = T) %>% head(n=250) %>% names()
cor.res <- covariate_cor(ps, covariate = "BMI_H1", taxa = taxa2test)
cor.res <- cbind(cor.res, ps@tax_table[rownames(cor.res),])
cor.res %>% arrange(-abs(rho)) %>% head()
##                rho      p.value     p.adjust  Kingdom     Phylum      Class
## Zotu132 -0.2087453 2.621122e-11 4.866350e-09 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia
## Zotu46  -0.2058279 4.991419e-11 4.866350e-09 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia
## Zotu80   0.2051104 5.839621e-11 4.866350e-09 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia
## Zotu176 -0.1892406 1.625381e-09 1.015863e-07 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia
## Zotu161 -0.1857467 3.257715e-09 1.628858e-07 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia
## Zotu35  -0.1693225 7.176606e-08 2.990253e-06 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia
##                 Order              Family                         Genus Species
## Zotu132 Clostridiales     Lachnospiraceae                     Roseburia    <NA>
## Zotu46  Clostridiales Christensenellaceae Christensenellaceae_R-7_group    <NA>
## Zotu80  Clostridiales     Lachnospiraceae             Lachnoclostridium    <NA>
## Zotu176 Clostridiales     Ruminococcaceae       Ruminococcaceae_UCG-002    <NA>
## Zotu161 Clostridiales     Ruminococcaceae Ruminococcaceae_NK4A214_group    <NA>
## Zotu35  Clostridiales     Ruminococcaceae       Ruminococcaceae_UCG-002    <NA>
psm <- psmelt(prune_taxa((cor.res %>% arrange(p.value) %>% head() %>% rownames()), ps))

ggplot(psm, aes(x=BMI_H1, y=Abundance)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~OTU+Genus) + 
  stat_cor() +
  scale_y_log10() + 
  geom_smooth() + 
  theme_bw() + 
  NULL

psm

## 4958 Christensenellaceae Christensenellaceae_R-7_group
##  [ reached 'max' / getOption("max.print") -- omitted 118 rows ]

2.4 Ancombc

3 Mutivariate

3.1 RF factor

Group="Sex"

ps.rf <- prune_samples(!is.na(get(Group, ps.genus@sam_data)), ps.genus)
if (taxa_are_rows(ps.rf)){
  otu_table(ps.rf) <- otu_table(t(  otu_table(ps.rf)), taxa_are_rows = F)
}

# Extract OTU/ASV table
otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps.rf))

# group prevelance 

group_dist <- get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data) %>% table()

group.prev <- list()
group.prev[["global"]] <- colSums(otu!=0)/nsamples(ps.rf)

if (class(get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data))!="numeric"){
  for (group in names(group_dist)){
  group.prev[[group]] <- colSums(otu[get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data) == group,]!=0)/group_dist[group]
  }
}

features.select <- unique(unlist(lapply(group.prev, function(x) names(which(x>0.05)))))

set.seed(123)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data), 
                                  p = 0.7, 
                                  list = FALSE)

rf_model <- ranger(
  x = otu[trainIndex,features.select],
  y = get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[trainIndex],
  num.trees = 1000,
  importance = "impurity",
  sample.fraction = c(0.5, 0.5),
  probability = TRUE,
  num.threads = parallel::detectCores()
)

print(rf_model)
## Ranger result
## 
## Call:
##  ranger(x = otu[trainIndex, features.select], y = get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[trainIndex],      num.trees = 1000, importance = "impurity", sample.fraction = c(0.5,          0.5), probability = TRUE, num.threads = parallel::detectCores()) 
## 
## Type:                             Probability estimation 
## Number of trees:                  1000 
## Sample size:                      701 
## Number of independent variables:  205 
## Mtry:                             14 
## Target node size:                 10 
## Variable importance mode:         impurity 
## Splitrule:                        gini 
## OOB prediction error (Brier s.):  0.2311599
pred <- predict(rf_model, otu[-trainIndex, features.select])
confusionMatrix(colnames(pred$predictions)[max.col(pred$predictions)] %>% factor(), get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex])
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   1   2
##          1  76  40
##          2  64 119
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.6522          
##                  95% CI : (0.5952, 0.7061)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5318          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1.638e-05       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.2943          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.02411         
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.5429          
##             Specificity : 0.7484          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6552          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.6503          
##              Prevalence : 0.4682          
##          Detection Rate : 0.2542          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3880          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6456          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : 1               
## 
library(pROC)

prob_class1 <- pred$predictions[, 1]
roc_obj <- roc(get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex], prob_class1)
auc(roc_obj)
## Area under the curve: 0.6792
plot(roc_obj)

## importance

importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model))
importance_df$Taxa <- rownames(importance_df)

# Sort by MeanDecreaseGini
importance_df <- importance_df %>%
  arrange(desc(importance(rf_model)))

top20 <- importance_df[1:20, ]
top20 <- cbind(top20, ps.rf@tax_table[top20$Taxa,])

psm <- psmelt(prune_taxa(top20$Taxa, ps.rf))

psm %>% 
  rename(Group = Group) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=Group, y=Abundance)) +
  #geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~OTU, scales="free") + 
  scale_y_log10() + 
  stat_compare_means() + 
  gghalves::geom_half_point() +
  gghalves::geom_half_violin() + 
  theme_bw() + 
  NULL

rankings <- psm %>%
  group_by(OTU) %>% mutate(rank=rank(Abundance)) %>% group_by(OTU, get(Group)) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(rank, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pivot_wider(values_from = mean_value, names_from = `get(Group)`)

taxa.effect.sign <- sign(unclass(unname(rankings[,2]-rankings[,3])[[1]]))
top20$importance <- unname(top20$`importance(rf_model)`*taxa.effect.sign[match(top20$Taxa,rankings$OTU)])

ggplot(top20, aes(x = reorder(Taxa, importance), 
                  y = importance, fill= Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Important Taxa",
       x = "Taxa",
       y = "Mean Decrease Gini")

3.2 RF continous

Group="FLI"
ps.rf <- prune_samples(!is.na(get(Group, ps.genus@sam_data)), ps.genus)

if (taxa_are_rows(ps.rf)){
  otu_table(ps.rf) <- otu_table(t(  otu_table(ps.rf)), taxa_are_rows = F)
}

# Extract OTU/ASV table
otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps.rf))

# group prevelance 

group_dist <- get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data) %>% table()

group.prev <- list()
group.prev[["global"]] <- colSums(otu!=0)/nsamples(ps.rf)

# if (class(get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data))!="numeric"){
#   for (group in names(group_dist)){
#     group.prev[[group]] <- colSums(otu[get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data) == group,]!=0)/group_dist[group]
#   }
# }

features.select <- unique(unlist(lapply(group.prev, function(x) names(which(x>0.05)))))

set.seed(123)

trainIndex <- createDataPartition(get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data), 
                                  p = 0.7, 
                                  list = FALSE)
rf_model <- ranger(
  x = otu[trainIndex,features.select],
  y = get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[trainIndex],
  num.trees = 1000,
  importance = "impurity",
  #sample.fraction = c(0.5, 0.5),
  num.threads = parallel::detectCores()
)

print(rf_model)
## Ranger result
## 
## Call:
##  ranger(x = otu[trainIndex, features.select], y = get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[trainIndex],      num.trees = 1000, importance = "impurity", num.threads = parallel::detectCores()) 
## 
## Type:                             Regression 
## Number of trees:                  1000 
## Sample size:                      700 
## Number of independent variables:  200 
## Mtry:                             14 
## Target node size:                 5 
## Variable importance mode:         impurity 
## Splitrule:                        variance 
## OOB prediction error (MSE):       688.633 
## R squared (OOB):                  0.1082327
pred <- predict(rf_model, otu[-trainIndex, features.select])
pred$predictions
##   [1] 39.80433 42.54393 32.77622 39.99967 39.16919 46.55223 41.79003 42.72951
##   [9] 51.72547 39.76282 48.66807 44.12976 34.09544 41.84171 46.66702 47.55240
##  [17] 42.21976 30.23286 42.47054 49.20484 47.70391 35.47618 46.14210 43.65499
##  [25] 49.68697 56.86087 24.56875 45.93190 22.46961 36.76014 39.89286 24.63301
##  [33] 36.26863 31.54690 28.57939 38.63375 49.24353 45.36209 33.50304 28.26379
##  [41] 29.50338 31.86913 40.28229 37.47213 36.42178 30.18128 41.70725 37.05593
##  [49] 24.16305 37.23444 29.94028 39.42694 48.10891 43.49258 42.42652 29.80134
##  [57] 39.90073 30.05278 35.20358 26.70804 45.47254 35.10561 33.09925 30.73937
##  [65] 45.59878 39.28688 26.78789 42.43984 33.02539 49.02082 44.39909 35.56069
##  [73] 46.60820 40.36766 55.17674 45.14883 45.77117 41.61213 42.64676 46.25436
##  [81] 37.95959 36.54793 44.05251 41.04852 47.47287 48.29474 31.35590 43.33208
##  [89] 33.05725 41.11591 40.70500 44.31005 47.39918 40.84989 40.80326 28.25867
##  [97] 51.93177 41.67937 46.15778 42.01677 39.27190 39.63599 33.32665 44.55402
## [105] 54.76004 28.85001 33.31343 41.54559 48.52485 40.46999 39.37468 50.01562
## [113] 43.10370 47.65153 40.72929 40.43939 47.00440 29.93941 27.52240 27.61399
## [121] 27.56990 46.13132 38.18345 43.80678 31.04343 38.05493 42.19694 39.06394
## [129] 31.93117 46.21637 36.30263 45.60194 47.46896 32.00810 45.46456 26.32668
## [137] 28.96199 37.49575 31.19092 31.84732 35.32049 26.91907 34.86758 31.23040
## [145] 29.08967 27.25295 33.04514 31.17982 41.32262 45.16060 37.61958 47.17577
## [153] 25.06488 36.38490 20.10600 42.91975 41.34824 41.03928 23.02760 28.99753
## [161] 44.43362 41.23720 45.08283 39.42773 48.99800 50.68940 41.34627 50.14461
## [169] 39.46765 38.42939 36.92719 44.74537 46.11611 39.25669 47.86218 50.68417
## [177] 37.90546 51.20125 46.66220 43.03696 46.25317 51.26243 36.02154 53.40581
## [185] 43.25964 36.08008 43.78198 32.16171 47.94882 27.10515 47.26220 44.38992
## [193] 39.18254 49.05557 33.60705 49.77315 39.03533 40.61100 29.85280 47.56796
## [201] 47.00169 48.98990 42.77427 50.10223 45.48611 51.42286 46.18771 37.15441
## [209] 48.90280 37.09959 35.15722 46.74223 32.01642 37.97016 47.13803 25.92922
## [217] 39.46743 27.34823 24.92454 27.89798 41.21401 21.83411 35.54765 33.67161
## [225] 36.38397 29.73508 32.80966 29.53290 25.94436 37.76263 28.75572 31.89046
## [233] 33.13491 24.67812 30.35569 33.45730 36.49005 29.36972 27.20345 35.01731
## [241] 28.75728 33.68681 50.99355 25.40150 29.98301 36.86502 27.32198 25.26941
## [249] 46.50205 28.66341 30.69570 29.18063 42.27627 35.45911 38.93130 40.81192
## [257] 30.30328 31.97140 29.59373 34.66184 39.49093 33.24532 49.50671 36.63695
## [265] 35.09433 49.54384 36.85622 31.95979 32.95569 32.84773 53.65419 39.73598
## [273] 33.90936 32.42935 49.78882 31.94488 45.83597 32.73336 41.89214 43.39825
## [281] 36.55944 37.50752 39.45484 31.43163 39.64600 48.05480 47.00535 45.97306
## [289] 46.00157 42.88926 35.35251 27.56960 46.92760 40.42282 44.96292 42.37957
## [297] 28.31921 45.07360 35.91816 30.29793
plot(pred$predictions, get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex])

df <- data.frame(FLI_predicted=pred$predictions, 
                 FLI=get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex])

ggplot(df,aes(x=FLI, y=FLI_predicted)) +
  geom_bin2d()

cor.test(pred$predictions, get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex])
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  pred$predictions and get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex]
## t = 4.1987, df = 298, p-value = 3.548e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1264789 0.3404639
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.2363348
r2 <- cor.test(pred$predictions, get(Group, ps.rf@sam_data)[-trainIndex])
r2$estimate^2
##        cor 
## 0.05585416
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model))
importance_df$Taxa <- rownames(importance_df)

# Sort by MeanDecreaseGini
importance_df <- importance_df %>%
  arrange(desc(importance(rf_model)))

top20 <- importance_df[1:20, ]
top20 <- cbind(top20, ps.rf@tax_table[top20$Taxa,])

psm <- psmelt(prune_taxa(top20$Taxa, ps.rf))

psm %>% 
  rename(Group = Group) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=Group, y=Abundance)) +
  facet_wrap(~OTU, scales="free") + 
  scale_y_log10() + 
  geom_point(alpha=0.2) + 
  stat_cor() + 
  NULL

cor.coef <- covariate_cor(ps = ps.rf, covariate = Group, taxa = top20$Taxa)
taxa.effect.sign <- sign(cor.coef$rho)[match(rownames(cor.coef),top20$Taxa)]

top20$`importance(rf_model)` <- top20$`importance(rf_model)`*taxa.effect.sign

ggplot(top20, aes(x = reorder(Taxa, `importance(rf_model)`), 
                  y = `importance(rf_model)`, fill= Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Important Taxa",
       x = "Taxa",
       y = "Mean Decrease Gini")
